import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut b = 0
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(x)
      b = x
    when None
      b = 0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
      i = i + 1
    else
      mut sign = 1
      if b == 45
        sign = -1
        i = i + 1
      else if b == 43
        i = i + 1
      end
      mut num = 0
      mut parsing = true
      while parsing
        if i >= n
          parsing = false
        else
          mut c = 0
          match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(x)
            c = x
          when None
            c = 0
          end
          if c >= 48 and c <= 57
            num = num * 10 + (c - 48)
            i = i + 1
          else
            parsing = false
          end
        end
      end
      result = list.push(result, num * sign)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(x)
    x
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  mut line1 = ""
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(x)
    line1 = x
  when None
    line1 = ""
  end
  mut line2 = ""
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(x)
    line2 = x
  when None
    line2 = ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  mut target = 0
  match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(x)
    target = x
  when None
    target = 0
  end
  let n = nums.len()
  mut found = false
  mut fi = 0
  mut fj = 0
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    if not found
      mut j = i + 1
      while j < n
        if not found
          let a = get_at(nums, i)
          let b = get_at(nums, j)
          if a + b == target
            found = true
            fi = i
            fj = j
          end
        end
        j = j + 1
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if found
    print("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
  else
    print("none")
  end
end
